decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a theoretical framework and assumptions for using recently vaccinated individuals as a negative control exposure in test-negative vaccine-effectiveness studies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework for interpreting this negative-control design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Repeats the vaccine-effect analysis using chlamydia as a negative control outcome.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of a negative control outcome in an infectious-disease vaccine study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses negative control methods to control unobserved risk factors when estimating protection from prior gastroenteritis episodes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to norovirus and sapovirus immunity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses maternal infection before pregnancy as a negative control exposure to assess residual familial confounding.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete negative-control application involving maternal infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Calibrates comparative vaccine-safety incidence-rate ratios using negative control outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application in multinational COVID-19 vaccine safety analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Employs a negative control outcome model to detect unmeasured confounding in an EV71 vaccination-program evaluation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to EV71-associated hand, foot, and mouth disease.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Conducts two negative-control studies using exposure periods outside pregnancy to investigate causality.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete negative-control cohort application including maternal infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents formal criteria for using negative control populations to rule out unmeasured confounding and direct causal effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of formal conditions for a negative-control design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Includes fracture and non-COVID-19 outcomes as negative controls to reveal likely unmeasured confounding in booster-effectiveness estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application in a COVID-19 booster-effectiveness study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses negative control cohorts and outcomes to investigate confounding in observational SARS-CoV-2 vaccine-effectiveness estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete nationwide vaccine-effectiveness application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses ankle or knee sprain and influenza vaccination as negative control outcomes to assess residual confounding.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application in comparative antibiotic-safety estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reanalyzes a zoster-vaccine safety study using a double-negative-control method combining negative control outcomes and exposures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete vaccine-safety application of double-negative controls.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops assumptions, models, and practical recommendations for negative control outcome adjustment in small randomized trials.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of NCO adjustment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Validates non-SARS-CoV-2 respiratory infection as a negative control outcome and evaluates its ability to detect bias in vaccine-effectiveness estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit empirical validation and bias-detection assessment of an NCO.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses breast cancer as a negative control outcome and reports null negative-control outcome and exposure estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application in evaluating a national HPV vaccination program.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Includes pneumococcal vaccination as an explicitly designated negative control outcome in the influenza-vaccination analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete negative-control validation in a vaccination study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses negative control outcomes to identify confounding and detection bias in post-COVID diabetes estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to post-acute SARS-CoV-2 outcomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses several negative control outcomes within a double negative control design to evaluate detection bias.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to SARS-CoV-2 infection and new-onset diabetes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses natural language processing, transformer-based language models, and clustering to map topics and detection methods across more than 44,000 AMR publications.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete NLP-based mapping of antimicrobial-resistance literature.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a propensity-adjusted national retrospective cohort to estimate comparative postoperative benefits and harms of antibiotic prophylaxis regimens.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete observational effect-estimation study of infection-prevention regimens.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a population-based nested case-control study and conditional logistic regression to estimate associations between cervical screening and cancer risk.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete observational evaluation of cervical screening and HPV-related cancers.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Defines target parameters, derives identifiability conditions, and proposes a novel inverse-probability estimator for test-negative designs with population controls.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for observational effect estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces the case time series design, supplies its modeling framework, and evaluates it through simulation and applications.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and validation of a new observational study design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses transmission simulations to show that traditional regression estimates from infectious-disease case data can be uninterpretable or misleading.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit simulation-based diagnosis of failures in observational risk-factor estimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a test-negative case-control design to estimate primary-series and booster vaccine effectiveness during Delta and Omicron periods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete observational vaccine-effectiveness application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a retrospective cohort with landmark-time regression to estimate mortality associations with delayed appropriate antimicrobial treatment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete observational effect-estimation study of bloodstream-infection treatment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses retrospective longitudinal cohort data and regression models to estimate risks associated with low-level HIV viraemia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete observational estimation of HIV treatment outcomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies the parametric g-formula to estimate the effect of antiretroviral therapy on AIDS or death and compares results with other g-methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological demonstration and assessment of the parametric g-formula.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation to estimate mortality under alternative ART-initiation thresholds while adjusting for time-dependent confounding.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV treatment-timing application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula on longitudinal HIV data to estimate population-intervention effects and contrast them with exposure effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological exposition of policy-relevant parametric g-formula estimands.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation alongside inverse weighting and targeted maximum likelihood to estimate a population-level contraceptive association.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application involving HIV-positive women and cervical precancer.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate mortality under observed and complete implementation of an ART-timing strategy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete tuberculosis and HIV treatment-policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to compare 10-year mortality under three ART-initiation thresholds.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV treatment-timing application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to adjust for baseline and time-varying confounding when comparing ART-initiation strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multinational HIV comparative-effectiveness application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation to estimate mortality and growth under different pediatric ART-initiation criteria.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pediatric HIV treatment application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate diarrheal incidence under hypothetical interventions preventing unnecessary infant antibiotic use.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete antibiotic-exposure and diarrheal-disease application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the g-formula to compare mortality and growth under alternative pediatric ART-initiation criteria.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multiregional pediatric HIV application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate per-protocol risks under immediate and deferred ART initiation in a randomized trial.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV treatment-effect application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a semiparametric g-formula approach to estimate pregnancy-loss risk under realistic smoking-cessation interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application among women with and without HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the g-computation algorithm to estimate how reducing community-level stigma would affect HIV-testing uptake.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-testing intervention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate five-year mortality under alternative ART-initiation strategies in older adults.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV treatment-timing application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate preventable proportions of intubation-associated pneumonia under ICU and care-bundle interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete healthcare-associated infection-prevention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric extended g-formula to estimate mortality under hypothetical ART and depression interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete intervention analysis among women with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate acquired HIV drug-resistance risks under alternative ART-initiation strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV drug-resistance application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a sensitivity-analysis approach for cause-of-death misclassification within the parametric g-formula.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension of the parametric g-formula.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a g-formula HIV treatment-timing analysis as the practical setting for evaluating bootstrap and multiple-imputation procedures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The methodological contribution concerns bootstrap inference, not development or guidance for the g-formula itself.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses parametric g-computation to estimate mortality effects of HCV infection and direct-acting antiviral treatment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV/HCV coinfection application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate mortality under alternative hepatitis C treatment-access policies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV/HCV treatment-policy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses G-computation with random survival forests to estimate survival differences after cancer diagnosis by HIV status.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application involving people with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate HIV-infection risk across young-woman and partner-age pairings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-risk application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses G-computation with random forests to estimate cancer-stage and treatment differences by HIV status.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application comparing people with HIV with a general-population sample.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses parametric g-computation to estimate mortality rates standardized by sex and age after ART initiation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV treatment-outcome application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Provides an implementation algorithm for parametric g-computation with dynamic baseline interventions and time-to-event outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological and implementation-oriented development of parametric g-computation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The abstract mentions g-computation as an extension, but the described simulation evaluates propensity-score and doubly robust estimators and does not clearly apply or evaluate g-computation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit development, application, or diagnostic assessment of the exact concept is established.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the g-formula to model individual and combined interventions on HIV risk factors and estimate resulting HIV incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-prevention intervention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses quantile g-computation for PFAS-mixture analysis, a distinct mixture-estimation method rather than the parametric g-formula/g-computation concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate cancer risks under immediate and deferred ART-initiation strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multinational HIV application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses parametric g-computation to estimate mortality impacts of hypothetical improvements in neighborhood poverty and deprivation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete intervention application among women with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a counterfactual-prediction approach based on the g-formula and develops prediction algorithms under hypothetical treatment strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension of g-formula reasoning to prediction across treatment settings.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses parametric g-computation to estimate rotavirus-vaccine seropositivity under hypothetical antibiotic-use interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete rotavirus vaccine application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to compare kidney outcomes under direct-acting antiviral initiation versus no initiation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete hepatitis C treatment application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate mortality under alternative ART-regimen switching strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV drug-resistance treatment application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses quantile g-computation for trace-element mixtures, which is distinct from the parametric g-formula/g-computation concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to impute sustained virological response status when post-treatment HCV-RNA testing is missing.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV/HCV treatment-response application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the g-formula to estimate the causal effect of in-person work on SARS-CoV-2 infection risk.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 occupational-risk application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses quantile-g-computation regression to combine deprivation-index components, a distinct mixture method rather than the parametric g-formula concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses parametric g-computation to estimate one-year mortality effects of hypertension among women receiving HIV treatment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV cohort application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation to estimate trachoma prevalence under hypothetical water, sanitation, and hygiene coverage interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete trachoma-elimination application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses regression standardization identified as the parametric g-formula to estimate severe COVID-19 risks under vaccination schedules.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 vaccination application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a novel inverse-probability weighted estimator of the generalized g-formula, not the named parametric g-formula or g-computation procedure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract supports a neighboring generalized-g-formula estimator rather than the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation with logistic regression to estimate standardized hypertension and statin-initiation risks for two PrEP regimens.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV pre-exposure prophylaxis safety application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explicitly uses a nonparametric g-formula, a neighboring estimator rather than the exact parametric g-formula concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No lineage role for the exact parametric concept is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies the g-formula alongside causal survival forests to evaluate dynamic antibiotic-switching policies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete MRSA treatment-strategy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to estimate mortality under hypothetical PM2.5 and CD4 scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application among people living with HIV.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation on logistic-regression models to estimate symptom risk differences after SARS-CoV-2 vaccination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 vaccine-safety application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses time-to-event quantile-based generalized g-computation for pollutant mixtures, a distinct mixture method rather than the parametric g-formula concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Extends the parametric g-formula to partial interference and proposes estimators for several population-level effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological extension with theory, simulation, and application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a g-computation model to examine the joint association of PFAS mixtures with chlamydia.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to chlamydia prevalence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies G-Net, a sequential framework for g-computation, to counterfactual fluid-treatment predictions in sepsis patients.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete sepsis treatment-strategy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents and evaluates a semiparametric Breslow estimator for g-computation with survival outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and simulation-based evaluation of a g-computation estimator.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops g-computation-type plug-in estimators for continuous longitudinal interventions under positivity limitations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development with simulations and an HIV application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation to estimate longitudinal risk differences for school environments and adolescent health outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application within an HIV-prevention cohort.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-formula methods to assess racial differences in timing of COVID-19 vaccine-series completion across urbanicity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 vaccination-disparity application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation to calculate causal concentration-response curves for pediatric efavirenz treatment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pediatric HIV pharmacologic application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses parametric g-computation to estimate site-specific effects of first-trimester malaria on pregnancy outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete malaria-in-pregnancy application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to standardize COVID-19 infection, hospitalization, and mortality estimates by race and dementia status.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 disparities application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses quantile g-computation for metal and trace-element mixtures, a distinct mixture-estimation method rather than the parametric g-formula concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses quantile g-computation for PFAS-mixture associations, not the exact parametric g-formula/g-computation method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses quantile g-computation for combined air-pollutant effects, a distinct mixture method rather than the parametric g-formula concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses G-computation as a robustness analysis for estimated effects of differentiated HIV service-delivery models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV service-delivery application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses marginal standardization identified as the parametric g-formula to estimate preeclampsia risk associated with maternal HIV.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete maternal-HIV application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the parametric g-formula to compare pregnancy risks under two contraceptive methods among women on dolutegravir-based ART.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV treatment and contraception application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses G-computation to estimate absolute long-COVID risks and differences under repeated infections and vaccination statuses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 reinfection application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses adjusted quantile g-computation for PFAS and metal mixtures, a distinct mixture method rather than the parametric g-formula concept.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No exact-concept lineage role is supported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses g-computation to estimate population-level effects of lottery incentives on gender-based violence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The method is concretely applied, but the primary outcome is violence rather than an infectious-disease outcome.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reviews the g-formula, its assumptions, modeling workflow, diagnostics, and comparative strengths and limitations relative to other g-methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit practical guidance for clinical and epidemiological use of the g-formula.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses number-needed-to-vaccinate analyses to evaluate the expected health impact and policy efficiency of an infant pertussis cocoon strategy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete policy-impact evaluation of pertussis immunization.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Performs a benefit-cost analysis and simulation of a national foot-and-mouth disease vaccination program.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete economic evaluation of infectious-disease control.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a decision-analytic economic model to estimate budget and health impacts of substituting Tdap for Td in wound management.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete budget-impact evaluation of a pertussis vaccination strategy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates village- and national-level financial impacts of foot-and-mouth disease outbreaks, including adjustment for under-reporting by simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete economic-impact evaluation of an infectious livestock disease.
